notes: The character creation at start wont display anything, after creation of a random char i did go back to main menu and could create a new char and see what was in there. but without the large address aware the game gets performance drop downs to unplayable. but after the fix the performance degradation is way smaller (Still it gets a drop on frames after an about and hour of gameplay
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: Character creation screen displays nothing, game is playable fine when character is created blindly, quite a lot of FPS performance, crashes with ESYNC disabled
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: Frequent crashes (every 20 or so minutes) even with Large Address Aware tweak. Performance is alright with occasional stutter. Hopefully future proton versions will correct this :(
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: The very beginning screen with character creation does not render, after that I had no issues. I played multiplayer with two of my friends for a few hours. There is some minor performance issues even with large address aware on. FPS rarely went below my monitor's 75Hz refresh rate and very little stuttering so it was not annoying. Very playable just needs the first scene to render.
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes:
The main menu and character creator only shows UI elements, not your character until you get into the game, then go back out to the menu.  So just create a random character, start the game, then quit back to the menu to create a more attractive character :)

Setting antialiasing to low leaves a halo around vegetation and a slight shimmer on water.  Leave it on at least medium.
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: Launched fine. Like another user pointed out you can not see your character in the creation screen, i just winged it. After that I was able to get into the game no issues with cut-scenes or npc interaction. Game runs well enough for me. Frames are bit sporadic at times. Some places I would get 120 others I would get 70. Occasional frame drops but nothing to severe. I think the game is completely playable as is but would be nice since it is a shooter to fix the frame drops that seem almost random.
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes:
With „PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE“ as start option game runs smooth - encountered no issues while playing for nearly one hour. 

Intro and vids not playing. 
Choose character screen and character generation are black. 

Some keybindings can only be changed at 
%(path_to_steam)/steamapps/compatdata/617290/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/Remnant/Saved/Config/WindowsNoEditor/GameUserSettings.ini%

(Had to change some settings because of using NEO2.0 as layout - inventory is fixed on „i” and not changeable from ingame menus).
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: Launched fine without tweaks, character didn't render during creation so I just randomed and started playing. 5 hours of near flawless gameplay so far, only noticed a few small rendering glitches. 1440p High stays over 60 almost all of the time, so not the best performance, but apparently that's just how the game is. Had one crash, but that also doesn't have to be Proton-related.
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: The character creation screen is completely black and so I just used a random look, however the game itself runs with pretty good frames with occasional drops, all in all playable imo
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: Runs perfectly minus a main menu rendering issue, which is resolved by exiting to main menu after playing on a character.
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: Updating my review on this -- after the latest proton release, crashes have gone away entirely. The game even has some Vulkan support with pre-caching available. With the large address aware fix mentioned in other reviews it runs great. Solid 60 FPS with very minor hangups. Super playable !
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: runs perfectly. The only issue is setting large address aware and the other issue is the character selection/creation screen that does not show the character. if that's fixed its a platinum. Played multiplayer for a few hours.
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

notes: I was able to play co-op for a few hours with no crashes. I referenced other reviewers and enabled "Large Address Aware" from the start. On the initial start, I still can't see my character on the main screen but I've never had an issue in game. One major issue I run into is that the the FPS will drop to <10 fps during level transitions which can be resolved by toggling fullscreen via settings (or i3wm) OR I have to restart the game.
title: Remnant: From the Ashes

for now is Bronze, not Borked just Bronze it seems to run well.
